text.title: Performance Budget Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Performance Budget Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Measure 30-day baseline (p50, p95, p99) BEFORE setting budget. Budgets without baseline are arbitrary.
rule.rationale: Baseline reveals natural variability; budget set on noise produces flaky CI.

rule.statement: Budget = baseline_p95 + 20% headroom. Tighter = flaky CI; looser = no enforcement.
rule.rationale: 20% cushion balances enforcement with day-to-day variance; industry empirical norm.

rule.statement: PR blocks on regression > budget. Override requires manager approval + documented reason.
rule.rationale: Soft warnings are ignored; hard blocks are enforced; manager override creates audit trail.

rule.statement: Budget per route or per critical path. Homepage budget ≠ admin dashboard budget.
rule.rationale: Global budgets either too strict for heavy pages or too loose for landing pages; per-route is the only honest design.

rule.statement: Quarterly: review budgets against current baseline. Slowdown = tighten; speedup = lower (don't widen back to old slack).
rule.rationale: Budgets without review ratchet up silently; quarterly review keeps them honest.
